sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: History tracks every code and documentation change for each indexed node. It's the audit trail that powers staleness detection, verification, and the pre-push review gate.

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
```bash
# View history for a specific node (via MCP tool)
# cortex_history(project_root, node_id)

# Mark a node as verified by a human
cortex mark-clean myproject::pm.database::get_engine . --reason 'Reformat only'

# Create a checkpoint (snapshot + prune old history)
cortex history checkpoint .

# List AI-verified nodes pending human review
cortex history agent-verified .
```

sections.heading: Core Functionality
sections.content:
- **Automatic recording:** Every `cortex build` automatically appends history rows when nodes change. No manual action needed.
- **Verification:** `cortex mark-clean` records a MANUAL_VERIFIED event. The MCP `cortex_mark_clean` tool records AGENT_VERIFIED.
- **Checkpointing:** `cortex history checkpoint` inserts a CHECKPOINT marker and prunes old rows, keeping the most recent N per node.
- **Pre-push gate:** `cortex history agent-verified` lists nodes where an AI agent approved the docs but a human hasn't reviewed yet.
- **Stale window tracking:** The MCP `cortex_history` tool annotates output with how many days the "stale window" has been open — the gap between a code change and the last doc update.

sections.heading: Configuration & Options
sections.content:
| Command | Option | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mark-clean` | `--reason TEXT` | Why the docs are still accurate | Empty |
| `history checkpoint` | `--keep N` | Rows to retain per node | 10 |
| `history checkpoint` | `--older-than N` | Only prune entries older than N days | 90 |
| `history checkpoint` | `--node-types TYPES` | Comma-separated types to apply to | `atomic_process,composite_process` |
| `history checkpoint` | `--no-prune` | Insert checkpoint without deleting rows | Off |

sections.heading: Best Practices & Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### Best Practices
- Run `cortex history checkpoint . --no-prune` before releases to snapshot state with git SHA
- Use `cortex history agent-verified .` as a pre-push check — review what the AI approved
- Provide meaningful `--reason` text when marking nodes clean for future audit

### Common Issues
- **Problem:** History table growing very large
- **Solution:** Run `cortex history checkpoint . --keep 5 --older-than 30` to prune aggressively
